name: Green or Red Enchiladas
recipeIngredient: 10 Flour or Corn Tortillas, 6-inches in diameter
recipeInstructions:
Servings: 8
1. Cook and stir onions in oil in a 3-quart saucepan until tender.
2. Stir in remaining ingredients except broth and sour cream. Cover and cook over medium heat for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
3. Transfer mixture to food processor work bowl fitted with steel blade or blender container; cover and process until smooth (about 1 minute).
4. Return mixture to saucepan; stir in broth. Heat to boiling; reduce heat.
5. Simmer uncovered for 10 minutes. Stir in sour cream. Cover and refrigerate any remaining sauce.
Tortillas should be warm when used.
6. Prepare basic green sauce and stir in the 1 cup of sour cream. Heat oven to 350°F (175°C).
7. Dip each tortilla into the sauce to coat both sides. Spoon 1/4 cup of shredded chicken onto each tortilla and roll up. Place seam sides down in a greased baking dish.
8. Pour remaining sauce over enchiladas and sprinkle with cheese. Bake, uncovered, until cheese is melted (about 15 minutes).
9. Serve warm with sour cream.
RED ENCHILADAS:
Substitute 2 cups of Basic Red Sauce for the Basic Green sauce.
Substitute shredded cheese or cooked beef for the chicken.
Basic Red Sauce
Servings: 4
8 Ancho Chilies
1 1/2 cups Warm Water
1/2 cup Onion, chopped
2 Garlic cloves, chopped
1/4 cup Vegetable Oil
8 oz Tomato Sauce
1 tbsp Oregano Leaves, Dried
1 tsp Cumin Seed
Salt (to taste)
Cover chilies with warm water. Let stand until softened, about 30 minutes. Drain.
Strain liquid; reserve.
Remove stems, seeds and membranes from chilies.
Cook and stir onion and garlic in oil in a saucepan until onion is tender.
Stir in chilies, reserved liquid and remaining ingredients. Heat to boiling, reduce heat.
Simmer uncovered for 20 minutes; cool.
Pour into food processor work bowl fitted with steel blade or blender container; cover and process until smooth.
Cover and refrigerate up to 10 days.
